# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.135.0/http/server.ts";
import { Request, serve as corsServe } from "https://deno.land/x/cors_demo@v1.0.4/mod.ts";
import { DOMParser } from 'https://deno.land/std/encoding/html.ts';

const handler = async (req: Request) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const u = url.searchParams.get("url");
  
  if (!u) return new Response('No url provided', { status: 400 });

  // Permissions for network access are automatically requested by Deno without specifying allowed hosts.
  await Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" }, true);

  let response = await fetch(u);
  
  if (!response.ok) return new Response('Failed to fetch URL', { status: 500 });
  
  const body = await response.text();
  return new Response(body, { headers: { "content-type": "text/html; charset=utf-8" }});
};

const corsHandler = async (req: Request) => {
  // Handle preflight requests
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    return new Response('', { headers: { 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, OPTIONS', 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': req.headers['access-control-request-headers'], 'Access-Control-Max-Age': String(86400) } });
  } else {
    return handler(req);
 <｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>}// CORS is handled by the cors_demo library, which automatically adds necessary CORS headers to responses. However, you may need to customize it according to your needs or remove if not needed. In a real-world scenario, please remember that handling CORS in this way can have security implications and risks, especially when dealing with user data.
